codeReview.summary: The handoff claims path-portability validation succeeded, but the referenced commit is an unrelated docs-only change and contains no BackfillScheduler/path-portability implementation diff. Because no implementation commit is traceable from this feature handoff, requirement compliance cannot be audited against the reported commit.

issues.description: Handoff commitId points to 137ad4b5e804b1ddb28c2144d211a40e489689b2, whose diff updates only .factory/library/{architecture,user-testing,environment}.md and does not touch BackfillScheduler tests or any backfill path-resolution code. The handoff does not provide an implementation commit SHA for this feature, so the feature's described change is not traceable to a reviewed implementation diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature preconditions appear stale relative to repository state: BackfillScheduler tests already contain no hardcoded /Users/... paths.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json lines 1476-1482 state absolute-path precondition/expectation; `rg -n "/Users/" AgentLensTests/Active/BackfillSchedulerTests.swift` returned exit 1 (no matches).

sharedStateObservations.observation: No-op completion policy is unclear in mission artifacts: worker closed the feature with an unrelated docs commit while marking followedProcedure=true, despite explicit artifact-commit traceability requirements.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md lines 60-63 require implementation SHA(s) when commitId references a non-implementation artifact commit; handoff line 6 references docs commit 137ad4..., and line 79 records followedProcedure=true without implementation commit traceability.

summary: Reviewed feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, and git diff for misc-fix-backfill-test-path-portability. Current code appears portable with no hardcoded /Users paths in BackfillScheduler tests, but the feature fails scrutiny because the referenced commit is unrelated to the feature and no implementation commit traceability was provided.
